title: Belgium vs Egypt: FIFA World Cup 2026 — where to watch, stadium, team news and everything we know so far | Business Upturn Sports
original_text:
Belgium vs Egypt: FIFA World Cup 2026 — where to watch, stadium, team news and everything we know so far
Belgium take on Egypt in the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group G opener at Lumen Field in Seattle, Washington. Kickoff is at 3:00 PM PT local time today, Monday June 15, which translates to 12:30 AM IST on Tuesday, June 16 for viewers in India. Belgium arrive as group favourites, unbeaten in their last 13 matches and powered by Kevin De Bruyne in what is widely expected to be his final World Cup. Egypt arrive led by Mohamed Salah — who turns 34 today — chasing the one achievement still missing from a glittering career: a World Cup knockout win for the Pharaohs. Group G also contains Iran and New Zealand.
Where to watch in India
TV (Hindi): Unite8 Sports 1 and Unite8 Sports 1 HD
TV (English): Unite8 Sports 2 and Unite8 Sports 2 HD
Live streaming: Zee5
Kickoff is at 12:30 AM IST on Tuesday, June 16. A late-night fixture for Indian audiences but one of the most compelling matches of the opening week — Salah’s World Cup debut for Egypt against a Belgium side with genuine title ambitions.
Stadium — Lumen Field, Seattle
Lumen Field is located at 800 Occidental Ave S, Seattle, Washington 98134 — in the heart of downtown Seattle, within walking distance of the waterfront. Home of the NFL’s Seattle Seahawks and MLS’s Seattle Sounders FC, the stadium is one of the loudest and most atmospheric venues in North American sport. For the 2026 FIFA World Cup, it has a capacity of approximately 68,740 seats and is being used as the official FIFA tournament venue referred to as Seattle Stadium. Lumen Field will host six FIFA World Cup 2026 matches in total, including a round of 32 knockout fixture. Local kickoff today is 12:00 PM PT — plan to arrive by 11:00 AM PT to allow time for security checks and entry. The stadium is accessible by light rail from downtown Seattle.
Coach: Rudi Garcia
Captain: Youri Tielemans
FIFA ranking: 9th
Belgium arrive at the 2026 World Cup on the back of an unbeaten 13-match run across all competitions, including seven unanswered goals across their final two warm-up victories over Croatia and Tunisia, according to GhanaWeb. Garcia, appointed in January 2025, has transformed a team that crashed out of Qatar 2022 at the group stage into a structured, confident attacking side with a clear tactical identity around a 4-2-3-1 formation.
Key players to watch:
Kevin De Bruyne (Napoli) — Belgium’s most important player and creative heartbeat at 34. After leaving Manchester City for Napoli, he arrives fit and expected to start every game as the number 10. His passing range, vision, and ability to control tempo are unmatched in this Belgium squad. Almost certainly his final World Cup, according to WorldCupPass.
Thibaut Courtois (Real Madrid) — Returns in goal after a much-publicised absence under the previous manager. One of the world’s best goalkeepers when fit and fully focused. His return transforms Belgium’s defensive confidence.
Jérémy Doku (Manchester City) — Belgium’s most dangerous wide player. No European player created more chances from open play during qualifying, contributing seven goals and assists across the campaign, according to GhanaWeb. His direct running and pace make him a constant threat against any full-back.
Amadou Onana (Aston Villa) — Dynamic defensive midfielder who provides the energy and pressing intensity behind De Bruyne. Shields the defence and drives forward effectively.
Leandro Trossard (Arsenal) — Expected to start on the left attacking side. Provides creativity and goal threat from wide areas.
Romelu Lukaku (Napoli) — Belgium’s all-time leading scorer with 89 international goals but carrying a major fitness question. Played barely an hour of competitive football this season due to recurring muscle injuries, according to WorldCupPass. Garcia named him on the strength of his experience and pedigree — but whether he starts or comes off the bench tonight remains unclear. Charles De Ketelaere (Atalanta) could start ahead of him if Garcia prioritises match sharpness, according to Squawka.
Notable omission: Malick Fofana, Belgium’s top scorer during qualifying, was controversially left out of the squad by Garcia, according to FIFAWorldCupNews.com.
Expected formation: 4-2-3-1, per Garcia’s established setup.
Expected XI (per Total Football Analysis): Courtois; Castagne, De Winter, Debast, De Cuyper; Onana, Tielemans; Doku, De Bruyne, Trossard; Lukaku
Team news — Egypt
Coach: Hossam Hassan
Captain: Mohamed Salah
FIFA ranking: 29th
Egypt return to the World Cup for the first time since 2018, led by their greatest ever player. Hossam Hassan — himself Egypt’s all-time leading scorer with 69 goals, a record Salah is now just two away from — has built a squad around a core of Al Ahly players supplemented by European-based talent. Eight of the 26-man squad come from Egyptian champions Al Ahly, giving the team strong domestic cohesion, according to WorldCupPass. Egypt’s CAF qualifying campaign was convincing — they qualified comfortably from a group containing Burkina Faso, Sierra Leone, and Guinea-Bissau, with Salah scoring nine times during the campaign, according to WorldCupPass.
Key players to watch:
Mohamed Salah (Liverpool) — Captain, talisman, and the heart of everything Egypt do. Turns 34 today. Scored nine goals in qualifying. Left Liverpool having recorded 380 goal involvements in 442 appearances, according to FIFAWorldCupNews.com. This is almost certainly his last World Cup and he will be determined to make it count.
Fitness concern: Salah suffered a hamstring injury in late April for Liverpool but played 45 minutes against Brazil in a June 6 friendly, according to Sports Mole. He is expected to be available tonight but may not start from the beginning — Hossam Hassan could manage his minutes to protect the hamstring.
Omar Marmoush (Manchester City) — Egypt’s second attacking star. A versatile, dynamic forward who enjoyed a breakthrough Premier League season and takes significant pressure off Salah. His ability to cut inside, deliver crosses, and create chances from wide positions gives Egypt a genuine second dimension in attack.
Emam Ashour (Al Ahly) — The engine of Egypt’s midfield. A powerful box-to-box player who combines defensive discipline with driving forward runs. His pressing intensity and energy will be central to how Egypt approach tonight’s match, according to FIFAWorldCupNews.com.
Mohamed Abdelmonem (Nice) — Organiser at the heart of Egypt’s defence. Provides composure and aerial quality from centre-back and is Egypt’s most significant European-based defensive player.
Mohamed El Shenawy (Al Ahly) — First-choice goalkeeper and one of the squad’s most experienced figures.
Hamza Abdelkarim (Barcelona B) — 18-year-old forward included as a wildcard selection. The most exciting young talent in Egypt’s squad, according to Al Jazeera, and a potential impact substitute tonight.
Expected formation: 4-3-3, per Total Football Analysis and Sports Mole predicted lineup.
Expected XI (per Sports Mole): El Shenawy; Hany, Abdelmonem, Rabia, Fatouh; Attia, Ashour, Hamdy Fathy; Salah, Marmoush, Zizo
Head-to-head record
Belgium and Egypt have met four times, all in friendlies — and Egypt hold a surprising advantage with three wins to Belgium’s one, according to Sports Mole. The results: Belgium 0-1 Egypt (March 1999), Egypt 4-0 Belgium (February 2005), Belgium 3-0 Egypt (June 2018), Belgium 1-2 Egypt (November 2022). This will be their first ever competitive fixture.
Group G context
Group G consists of Belgium, Egypt, Iran, and New Zealand. Belgium are the clear favourites to top the group. Egypt are the most credible challenger for second place, though Iran — ranked higher than Egypt — will also be targeting the knockout rounds. New Zealand arrive as strong underdogs. A Belgium win tonight would put them in firm control of Group G heading into Matchday 2 against Iran.
Everything else you need to know
All FIFA World Cup 2026 tickets are 100% digital and must be accessed via the official FWC26 Mobile Tickets app. Screenshots and printouts will not be accepted. There will be no over-the-counter ticket sales at Lumen Field on matchday, according to Goal.com — all purchases must be completed online before arriving at the venue. Last-minute resale tickets are available on StubHub, TickPick, SeatGeek, and Vivid Seats. Entry-level tickets are currently listed from $410 on StubHub (corner 300-level seats, per MyNorthwest) and from $565 on TickPick. All ticket transfers must be accepted at least 60 minutes before kickoff, per Ticketmaster’s official listing.
